seed_problem: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: replay the online spot/on-demand/pause decision as a STATIC full-trace optimization AND couple many ships through a shared per-hour spot capacity while adding a per-run gantry fixed charge, turning the poly-time single-job replay into an NP-hard fixed-charge scheduling problem where greedy / per-ship DP / local search diverge.
title: Slack Tide Quay: Spot-vs-Shore Crane Power Replay
strategies: trivial baseline: every ship works its earliest w_j hours contiguously on shore power (one run each)
spot-chasing greedy: pick each ship's w_j cheapest-spot hours, grab a shared spot slot if one remains else shore; ignores gantry re-mobilisation and fragments runs
per-ship fixed-charge DP: tight-window-first, dynamic program per ship trading spot/shore price against G-per-run gantry cost under the shared remaining spot capacity
capacity-aware local search: reassign spot slots and merge/split runs across ships to reduce total power + gantry cost
